title: INSERT & Returning

glossary.definition: The process of getting data back from the database after it has been stored.
Stating something clearly and directly, leaving no room for confusion or guessing.

overview.what: INSERT adds new rows to a table. By using the RETURNING clause, you can immediately get back the data that was inserted, which is incredibly useful for retrieving auto-generated IDs or default values without needing a second SELECT query. This acts as a single-step row addition and retrieval.
overview.why: Every application needs to create data. Using RETURNING prevents race conditions and saves a database round-trip when you need the newly created record's details.

why_learning_this: You can insert single or multiple rows in one command.
You can retrieve newly generated primary keys instantly to use them as foreign keys in other tables.

common_mistakes.title: Column order mismatch
common_mistakes.explanation: Providing VALUES in a different order than the column list. Always explicitly list columns to ensure values match.

common_mistakes.title: Missing required fields
common_mistakes.explanation: Failing to provide a value for a column that does not have a default and cannot be NULL (like primary keys or required names).

recall_questions.q: What is the benefit of adding the RETURNING clause to an INSERT statement?
recall_questions.answer: It returns the inserted row(s) immediately, avoiding a separate SELECT query to fetch auto-generated IDs or default values.

understanding_checks.query: INSERT INTO customers (id, name, country, signup_date) VALUES (9, 'Ian', 'CA', '2023-09-01') RETURNING id, name;
understanding_checks.question: What rows does this query return?
understanding_checks.why: The INSERT statement adds the new row, and RETURNING id, name projects only those two columns for the newly inserted row.

understanding_checks.query: INSERT INTO products (id, name, category, price) VALUES (7, 'Tablet', 'Electronics', 299.99), (8, 'Lamp', 'Furniture', 45.00) RETURNING id, name;
understanding_checks.question: What rows does this query return?
understanding_checks.why: Multiple rows are inserted, and RETURNING gives back the id and name for both newly created rows.

query_walkthrough.query: INSERT INTO products (id, name, category, price) VALUES (9, 'Pen', 'Stationery', 2.99) RETURNING id, name, price;
query_walkthrough.focus: The immediate projection of the inserted row via the RETURNING clause.

practice_tasks.title: Insert a new order
practice_tasks.prompt: Given this incomplete INSERT statement, modify it to add order ID 9 for customer 1 on '2023-09-10' with status 'pending', and return all columns of the new order.

challenge.title: Insert and Return
challenge.prompt: Write a query to insert a new product: id 10, name 'Headphones', category 'Electronics', price 99.99. Return the name and category of the inserted product.
challenge.solution: INSERT INTO products (id, name, category, price) VALUES (10, 'Headphones', 'Electronics', 99.99) RETURNING name, category;
